# Contract: SBI Reader FFI (UniFFI Swift boundary)

**Feature**: `004-sbi-cc-parser` | **Date**: 2026-07-15
**Module**: `kaname-core` (Rust) → `KanameCore` (Swift, generated by UniFFI)

Additive to the P1 + ICICI + HDFC `engine-ffi` contracts. The "external interface" is the exported
UniFFI surface (not an HTTP API); generated Swift signatures are the consumer-facing form.
**Reuses** the existing `Decimal`/`NaiveDate` custom types, the `Direction` enum, and the
`ParsedStatement`/`ParsedTransaction` records **unchanged** — SBI adds **two functions and no new
type**.

---

## Exported functions

### `read_sbi_statement`

| | |
|-|-|
| Rust | `#[uniffi::export] pub fn read_sbi_statement(lines: Vec<String>, full_text: String) -> ParsedStatement` |
| Swift | `public func readSbiStatement(lines: [String], fullText: String) -> ParsedStatement` |

**Contract**
- Parses **already-extracted** text `lines` (one statement row per element) + `full_text` (used
  only for enrichment). **Never** opens a file or PDF (FR-015).
- **Single layout**: `DD Mon YY <details> <amount> C|D`. Produces one `ParsedTransaction` per
  matching row; non-matching lines (headers/summaries/balances/totals) are ignored (FR-005); rows
  matching the shape but failing date/amount parse go to `ParsedStatement.errored_lines` (FR-014) —
  **never throws, never aborts**. A statement with no matching rows returns empty `lines` with no
  error.
- **Direction from the terminal `C`/`D` marker**: `C → .credit`, `D → .debit` (via
  `classify(desc, dir, None)`) — never the amount's sign or the description's wording; the marker
  wins even when the description contains a credit/debit word (FR-008/009, SC-004).
- **Amount** is an exact non-negative `Decimal` with scale preserved; Indian thousands grouping is
  stripped (FR-006/007, SC-005). Money never crosses as `f64`.
- Enriches `period_start` + `period_end` (both parsed from the `Statement Period: <from> to <to>`
  line) and `card_last4` (masked PAN via the `Credit Card Number` anchor) from `full_text` when
  present; absent → left `nil`. When the mask exposes **fewer than four** trailing digits,
  `card_last4` is **`nil`** (never fabricated) (FR-010/011/012/013).
- **Pure & deterministic**: identical input ⇒ identical output; no network, clock, locale, or global
  state (FR-016/019). Total function — no failure surfaced to Swift.

**Consumer expectations (app/test)**
- Call with PDFKit-extracted lines + full text. Treat `errored_lines` as review items; still consume
  every `lines` entry. Do not infer direction from `amount` — use `direction`. Treat `cardLast4 ==
  nil` as "unknown", never as a value to fabricate.

### `sbi_claims`

| | |
|-|-|
| Rust | `#[uniffi::export] pub fn sbi_claims(full_text: String) -> bool` |
| Swift | `public func sbiClaims(fullText: String) -> Bool` |

**Contract**
- Returns `true` iff `full_text` contains an SBI Card issuer marker (`"SBI Card"` or
  `"GSTIN of SBI Card"`, case-insensitive) — the document-plausibility gate (FR-001).
- MUST return `false` for other issuers' statements (e.g. an ICICI or HDFC document) so a statement
  is never misattributed to SBI (FR-002, US1-AC4). Pure, deterministic, no I/O.

> Internally: `read_sbi_statement` wraps `read_lines(&SbiReader, …)` (single layout — **not** the
> HDFC composite); `sbi_claims` delegates to `claims(&SbiReader, …, "SBI_CARD")`. `lib.rs` re-exports
> both functions (as it does the ICICI and HDFC pairs); `statement/mod.rs` gains `pub mod sbi;`.

---

## Types (all reused — no new type)

- `record ParsedStatement` / `record ParsedTransaction` — **unchanged** from the ICICI/HDFC slices.
  SBI **populates `periodStart`** (like HDFC) and may leave `cardLast4 = nil`.
- `enum Direction { Debit, Credit }` → Swift `.debit` / `.credit` (P1, `uniffi::Enum`).
- Custom types `Decimal`↔`String`↔`Foundation.Decimal` and `NaiveDate`↔ISO-8601 `String`
  (P1, in `ffi.rs` + `uniffi.toml`) — **unchanged**. Money never crosses as `f64`.

> Swift shape (for reference, unchanged): `ParsedStatement { bankCode: String; lines:
> [ParsedTransaction]; erroredLines: [String]; periodStart: String?; periodEnd: String?;
> cardLast4: String?; confidence: Double }`.

---

## Contract tests (test-first — FR-022/024)

**Rust (`cargo test`)** — engine-side truth (`tests/parity.rs` + `sbi.rs` unit tests):
- **Golden parity**: `read_sbi_statement(basic.lines, basic.full_text)` equals the fixture's
  `expected` exactly — rows `2026-04-21 / 643.00 / Credit / INR / "CARD CASHBACK CREDIT"` and
  `2026-05-20 / 82900.00 / Debit / INR / "APPLE INDIA STORE MUMBAI IN"`; `period_start 2026-04-22`;
  `period_end 2026-05-21`; **`card_last4` null**; `errored_lines` empty (SC-001/003/009).
- **Determinism**: two calls on the same input are equal.
- **Wrong issuer**: `sbi_claims("ICICI Bank …") == false`, `sbi_claims("HDFC Bank Credit Cards") ==
  false`; `sbi_claims(basic.full_text) == true` (FR-002, SC-002).
- **Direction from marker**: a row whose description contains "CREDIT" but whose terminal marker is
  `D` classifies **Debit** (marker beats wording); `C` → Credit (SC-004).
- **Malformed row**: a line matching the SBI shape but with an unparseable date lands in
  `errored_lines`, valid rows still returned, no panic (reuses the existing seam behavior).

**Swift (Swift Testing, `import KanameCore`)** — "core ↔ Swift SBI parse":
- `readSbiStatement(...)` over the two golden lines → 2 `lines`; `lines[0]` = `2026-04-21`,
  `Decimal(string:"643.00")`, `.credit`, `"INR"`, `"CARD CASHBACK CREDIT"`; `lines[1]` =
  `2026-05-20`, `Decimal(string:"82900.00")`, `.debit`, `"APPLE INDIA STORE MUMBAI IN"`.
- `periodStart == "2026-04-22"`; `periodEnd == "2026-05-21"`; **`cardLast4 == nil`**.
- `sbiClaims(fullText:) == true` for the SBI text and `false` for an ICICI/HDFC string.
- Amounts compared as `Foundation.Decimal` value-equality (exact; never float).

---

## Stability / compatibility
- Adding two functions is additive; reusing the records means **no wire-format change** and no new
  Swift type.
- The generated `Generated/kaname_core.swift` + `KanameCoreFFI.xcframework` are build artifacts,
  rebuilt by `make core-xcframework` (which runs **before** `tuist generate`). **This contract**,
  not the generated files, is the reviewed source of truth.
